target.name: Mammoth Biosciences
target.founder: Jennifer Doudna, Trevor Martin, Janice Chen, Lucas Harrington

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences is a Series D-stage biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and others, having raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in Biotech & Health, including Abridge ($5.3B Series E), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B Series C), Insitro ($2.4B Series C), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B Series C).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method uses the $600M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.5x Series D step-up multiplier, implying a valuation of $900M. The peer-median method anchors to the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $3.2B (range: $1.5B–$5.3B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.1x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $765M (low) to $3.7B (high), with a median implied value of $2.0B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Mammoth's stage and development progress align closer to earlier-stage private peers or more advanced comparables like Abridge. Key sensitivities include path to near-term revenue disclosure and clinical/regulatory progress relative to the peer cohort.
